criteria.description: The in-app SBTI flow now localizes the external question bank, type library, scoring logic, and persona artwork into the repo instead of depending on runtime third-party assets.
criteria.notes: The question set, dimension metadata, type mappings, and fallback rules were ported from sbti.unun.dev, while persona posters are served from local static assets under public/sbti/images.

criteria.description: Users can complete the SBTI assessment inside the landing page with a responsive overlay that behaves like a desktop modal and a mobile full-screen page, then see the matching persona artwork and summary.
criteria.notes: The assessment panel owns question progression, result rendering, and responsive layout rules; the integration suite covers opening the panel, completing a real answer path, and closing it with a valid result.

criteria.description: Finishing the assessment autofills the Hero SBTI gate and keeps the existing Journey guardrails intact, with regression tests and the production build both passing.
criteria.evidence: src/App.tsx + src/components/sections/HeroSection.tsx + npm run test + npm run build
criteria.notes: The result callback normalizes and writes the returned SBTI code back into the Hero input, preserves focus/error behavior, and the final verification completed with 37 passing tests plus a successful Vite build.
